codeReview.summary: The foundation review docs satisfy VAL-ARCH-001: README, architecture overview, and dependency map are present, code-grounded, and clearly cover module boundaries, controller surfaces, shared infrastructure, cross-module dependencies, and coupling hotspots. The only notable concern is that the commit bundled review-mission scaffolding under `.factory/` in addition to the requested docs, which was additive and helpful but outside the feature brief's docs-only surface.

issues.description: The feature commit bundled mission bootstrap changes in `.factory/init.sh`, `.factory/services.yaml`, `.factory/library/*`, and `.factory/skills/review-worker/SKILL.md` alongside the requested architecture docs. These additions were additive and are currently useful, but they exceeded the feature's stated docs-only deliverables and blurred feature output versus mission setup.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Review-only mission scaffolding was bootstrapped by the architecture foundation feature instead of being fully provisioned before feature execution. Future review missions should either pre-seed the `.factory/` review scaffolding or explicitly permit the bootstrap files in the foundation feature scope.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit `b01b2dd1a5c2ef773a0986b9d1dc70d299d25c0b` changes `.factory/init.sh`, `.factory/services.yaml`, `.factory/library/review-playbook.md`, `.factory/library/user-testing.md`, and `.factory/skills/review-worker/SKILL.md` in addition to `docs/code-review/README.md`, `architecture-overview.md`, and `dependency-map.md`.

summary: PASS. The architecture foundation docs meet the milestone's coverage requirements and provide usable code-grounded review scaffolding; the only issue is a non-blocking scope bleed where mission bootstrap files under `.factory/` were committed together with the docs.
